# homebutler

> Single-binary homelab management CLI written in Go. Monitor servers, manage Docker containers, install self-hosted apps, Wake-on-LAN, network scanning, alerts, backup/restore, and multi-server SSH — all from one 15MB binary with zero dependencies.

## What it does
- System monitoring: CPU, memory, disk usage, uptime across multiple servers
- Docker management: list, restart, stop, logs, stats (per-container resource usage)
- One-command app install: uptime-kuma, vaultwarden, filebrowser, it-tools, gitea
- Wake-on-LAN: wake machines by MAC address or configured name
- Network scanning: discover devices on local network (IP, MAC, hostname)
- Open ports: list listening ports with process info
- Alerts: threshold-based monitoring with continuous watch mode
- Backup/restore: Docker volume backup and restore
- Multi-server: manage remote servers over SSH from a single CLI
- Built-in MCP server: 15 AI-compatible tools for Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc.
- Web dashboard: real-time browser UI with go:embed
- TUI: terminal dashboard with Bubble Tea

## How it compares
- vs Portainer: homebutler is CLI-first, single binary, no Docker required to run
- vs CasaOS: homebutler is not an OS, just a lightweight CLI tool
- vs btop/htop: homebutler adds Docker, multi-server, app install, and AI integration
- vs Komodo: homebutler is simpler, single binary, no database required

## Install
brew install Higangssh/homebutler/homebutler
# or
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Higangssh/homebutler/main/install.sh | sh

## Quick start
homebutler init                    # interactive setup
homebutler status --all            # all servers at once
homebutler docker list             # list containers
homebutler install uptime-kuma     # deploy an app
homebutler docker stats            # container resource usage
homebutler serve                   # web dashboard

## Links
- GitHub: https://github.com/Higangssh/homebutler
- MCP Server: https://glama.ai/mcp/servers/Higangssh/homebutler
